---
title: "Chapter 15 - Purushottama Yoga"
---

<div class="chapter-title-sanskrit">पुरुषोत्तमयोगः</div>

<nav class="chapter-nav"><a href="chapter-14.html">Previous chapter</a><a href="chapter-16.html">Next chapter</a></nav>

<div class="verse-list">
<article class="verse" id="v-15-1" data-reference="15.1">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-15-1">15.1</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">They speak of an imperishable ashvattha tree, its roots above and branches below, whose leaves are the hymns; he who knows it knows the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." aria-label="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." title="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." data-glossary-id="veda">Vedas</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">ऊर्ध्वमूलमधःशाखमश्वत्थं प्राहुरव्ययम् ।<br>
छन्दांसि यस्य पर्णानि यस्तं वेद स वेदवित् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-15-2" data-reference="15.2">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-15-2">15.2</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Its branches spread below and above, nourished by the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." aria-label="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." title="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." data-glossary-id="guna">gunas</span>, with the objects of sense for buds; and below, its roots stretch on, binding to action in the world of men.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अधश्चोर्ध्वं प्रसृतास्तस्य शाखा<br>
गुणप्रवृद्धा विषयप्रवालाः ।<br>
अधश्च मूलान्यनुसन्ततानि<br>
कर्मानुबन्धीनि मनुष्यलोके ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-15-3" data-reference="15.3">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-15-3">15.3</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Its form is not perceived here as it is—no end, no beginning, no resting place. Having cut down this firm-rooted ashvattha with the strong axe of non-attachment,</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">न रूपमस्येह तथोपलभ्यते<br>
नान्तो न चादिर्न च सम्प्रतिष्ठा ।<br>
अश्वत्थमेनं सुविरूढमूलं<br>
असङ्गशस्त्रेण दृढेन छित्त्वा ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-15-4" data-reference="15.4">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-15-4">15.4</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">one must then seek that place from which, having gone, none return again. &#x27;I take refuge in that primal person, from whom the ancient unfolding streamed forth.&#x27;</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">ततः पदं तत्परिमार्गितव्यं<br>
यस्मिन्गता न निवर्तन्ति भूयः ।<br>
तमेव चाद्यं पुरुषं प्रपद्ये ।<br>
यतः प्रवृत्तिः प्रसृता पुराणी ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-15-5" data-reference="15.5">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-15-5">15.5</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Free of pride and delusion, the fault of attachment conquered, ever dwelling in the self, desire stilled, released from the pairs of opposites known as pleasure and pain, the undeluded reach that imperishable place.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">निर्मानमोहा जितसङ्गदोषा<br>
अध्यात्मनित्या विनिवृत्तकामाः ।<br>
द्वन्द्वैर्विमुक्ताः सुखदुःखसंज्ञै-<br>
र्गच्छन्त्यमूढाः पदमव्ययं तत् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-15-6" data-reference="15.6">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-15-6">15.6</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Neither sun nor moon nor fire lights up that place; having gone there, they do not return—that is my supreme abode.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">न तद्भासयते सूर्यो न शशाङ्को न पावकः ।<br>
यद्गत्वा न निवर्तन्ते तद्धाम परमं मम ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-15-7" data-reference="15.7">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-15-7">15.7</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">A portion of myself, become an eternal living soul in the world of the living, draws to itself the senses, with the mind as the sixth, that rest in <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="prakriti: Nature, material process, or the field of change and manifestation." aria-label="prakriti: Nature, material process, or the field of change and manifestation." title="prakriti: Nature, material process, or the field of change and manifestation." data-glossary-id="prakriti">prakriti</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">ममैवांशो जीवलोके जीवभूतः सनातनः ।<br>
मनःषष्ठानीन्द्रियाणि प्रकृतिस्थानि कर्षति ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-15-8" data-reference="15.8">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-15-8">15.8</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">When it takes on a body, and when it departs from one, it carries these along, as the wind carries scents from their places.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">शरीरं यदवाप्नोति यच्चाप्युत्क्रामतीश्वरः ।<br>
गृहीत्वैतानि संयाति वायुर्गन्धानिवाशयात् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-15-9" data-reference="15.9">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-15-9">15.9</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Presiding over hearing, sight, touch, taste, and smell, and over the mind, it experiences the objects of sense.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">श्रोत्रं चक्षुः स्पर्शनं च रसनं घ्राणमेव च ।<br>
अधिष्ठाय मनश्चायं विषयानुपसेवते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-15-10" data-reference="15.10">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-15-10">15.10</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The deluded do not see it as it departs, abides, or experiences, joined with the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." aria-label="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." title="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." data-glossary-id="guna">gunas</span>; those with the eye of knowledge see it.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">उत्क्रामन्तं स्थितं वापि भुञ्जानं वा गुणान्वितम् ।<br>
विमूढा नानुपश्यन्ति पश्यन्ति ज्ञानचक्षुषः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-15-11" data-reference="15.11">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-15-11">15.11</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The yogins who strive see it seated in the self; but the witless, their selves untrained, do not see it, even though they strive.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यतन्तो योगिनश्चैनं पश्यन्त्यात्मन्यवस्थितम् ।<br>
यतन्तोऽप्यकृतात्मानो नैनं पश्यन्त्यचेतसः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-15-12" data-reference="15.12">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-15-12">15.12</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The light that dwells in the sun and lights up the whole world, and that which is in the moon and in fire—know that light to be mine.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यदादित्यगतं तेजो जगद्भासयतेऽखिलम् ।<br>
यच्चन्द्रमसि यच्चाग्नौ तत्तेजो विद्धि मामकम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-15-13" data-reference="15.13">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-15-13">15.13</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">And entering the earth, I uphold all beings by my power; becoming the moon, full of sap, I nourish all the plants.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">गामाविश्य च भूतानि धारयाम्यहमोजसा ।<br>
पुष्णामि चौषधीः सर्वाः सोमो भूत्वा रसात्मकः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-15-14" data-reference="15.14">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-15-14">15.14</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Becoming the fire of digestion, dwelling in the bodies of living beings, joined with the inward and outward breaths, I digest the four kinds of food.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अहं वैश्वानरो भूत्वा प्राणिनां देहमाश्रितः ।<br>
प्राणापानसमायुक्तः पचाम्यन्नं चतुर्विधम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-15-15" data-reference="15.15">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-15-15">15.15</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">I am seated in the heart of all; from me come memory, knowledge, and their loss;<br>
I am what is to be known through all the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." aria-label="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." title="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." data-glossary-id="veda">Vedas</span>; I am the author of the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Vedanta: The end or culmination of the Vedas; in the text, the knowledge taught by the Vedic tradition." aria-label="Vedanta: The end or culmination of the Vedas; in the text, the knowledge taught by the Vedic tradition." title="Vedanta: The end or culmination of the Vedas; in the text, the knowledge taught by the Vedic tradition." data-glossary-id="vedanta">Vedanta</span> and the knower of the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." aria-label="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." title="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." data-glossary-id="veda">Vedas</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">सर्वस्य चाहं हृदि सन्निविष्टो<br>
मत्तः स्मृतिर्ज्ञानमपोहनञ्च ।<br>
वेदैश्च सर्वैरहमेव वेद्यो<br>
वेदान्तकृद्वेदविदेव चाहम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-15-16" data-reference="15.16">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-15-16">15.16</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">There are two persons in the world, the perishable and the imperishable: the perishable is all beings, the unchanging is called the imperishable.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">द्वाविमौ पुरुषौ लोके क्षरश्चाक्षर एव च ।<br>
क्षरः सर्वाणि भूतानि कूटस्थोऽक्षर उच्यते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-15-17" data-reference="15.17">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-15-17">15.17</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">But there is another, the supreme person, called the supreme <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Atman: True Self, Soul, or inner principle; kept distinct from ordinary ego or individual personality." aria-label="Atman: True Self, Soul, or inner principle; kept distinct from ordinary ego or individual personality." title="Atman: True Self, Soul, or inner principle; kept distinct from ordinary ego or individual personality." data-glossary-id="atman">Atman</span>, the imperishable lord who enters the three worlds and sustains them.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">उत्तमः पुरुषस्त्वन्यः परमात्मेत्युदाहृतः ।<br>
यो लोकत्रयमाविश्य बिभर्त्यव्यय ईश्वरः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-15-18" data-reference="15.18">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-15-18">15.18</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Since I surpass the perishable and am higher even than the imperishable, I am renowned in the world and in the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." aria-label="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." title="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." data-glossary-id="veda">Vedas</span> as <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Purushottama: The Supreme Person: epithet of Krishna indicating the supreme reality that transcends both the perishable and the imperishable" aria-label="Purushottama: The Supreme Person: epithet of Krishna indicating the supreme reality that transcends both the perishable and the imperishable" title="Purushottama: The Supreme Person: epithet of Krishna indicating the supreme reality that transcends both the perishable and the imperishable" data-glossary-id="purushottama">Purushottama</span>, the supreme person.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यस्मात्क्षरमतीतोऽहमक्षरादपि चोत्तमः ।<br>
अतोऽस्मि लोके वेदे च प्रथितः पुरुषोत्तमः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-15-19" data-reference="15.19">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-15-19">15.19</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">He who, undeluded, thus knows me as the supreme person knows all; he worships me with his whole being, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." aria-label="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." title="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." data-glossary-id="bharata">Bharata</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यो मामेवमसम्मूढो जानाति पुरुषोत्तमम् ।<br>
स सर्वविद्भजति मां सर्वभावेन भारत ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-15-20" data-reference="15.20">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-15-20">15.20</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Thus I have taught you this most secret teaching, O blameless one; knowing it, one becomes wise, his task accomplished, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." aria-label="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." title="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." data-glossary-id="bharata">Bharata</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">इति गुह्यतमं शास्त्रमिदमुक्तं मयानघ ।<br>
एतद्बुद्ध्वा बुद्धिमान्स्यात्कृतकृत्यश्च भारत ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

</div>
